request: When a contract is signed, we want an invoice to be created automatically.

problem.value: Avoid manual, delayed or forgotten invoicing after a contract is signed; reduce lag between signature and billing.
problem.evidence: 'When a contract is signed, we want an invoice created automatically' implies a manual/delayed step today.

current_process.value: Invoices are likely created manually by finance/admin after being told a contract is signed.
current_process.evidence: No process described; inferred from the automation request itself.

actors.value: Likely: system triggers on signature event; finance/admin reviews or validates the generated invoice.
actors.evidence: Not stated; inferred from domain norms (invoices are rarely auto-finalized without review).

business_objects.value: Contract to Invoice link exists, but amount source, numbering sequence, VAT and currency are unspecified.
business_objects.evidence: Context: 'an invoice is rarely just created — amount, numbering, billing rules must come from somewhere.'

business_rules.value: Unknown whether every contract gets exactly one invoice, or billing is milestone-based/recurring/partial (one-to-many).
business_rules.evidence: Context: 'A contract is one-to-many with its invoices (milestones, recurring, partial billing) — invoice the contract is rarely a single document.'

workflow.value: Unclear if invoice creation is synchronous (blocking, user sees failure) or asynchronous (queued, pending state), and how duplicate triggers (re-signature, amendment) are prevented.
workflow.evidence: Context: 'create X automatically hides which exact trigger, idempotency, sync vs async — different builds.'

integrations.value: Who/what is notified once the invoice is created (client contact, accounting system) is unspecified.
integrations.evidence: Not mentioned; inferred need from domain notes on notification routing.

permissions.value: Unknown who can view, edit, validate or cancel the auto-generated invoice.
permissions.evidence: Context: 'permissions are forgotten until acceptance testing.'

config_vs_custom.value: Unclear whether this trigger/rule is standard for all clients or configurable per client/contract type.
config_vs_custom.evidence: Context: 'Client-specific: approval circuits, business rules, notification routing' — billing rule likely varies.

constraints.value: French VAT and legal invoice numbering rules apply.
constraints.evidence: Context: 'invoicing follows French VAT and legal numbering rules.'

edge_cases.value: Unhandled: contract amended after signature, contract cancelled post-signature, incomplete billing data at signature time, re-signature triggering duplicate invoice.
edge_cases.evidence: Not mentioned; inferred from context's amendment/idempotency traps.

reporting.value: No stated need yet, but invoice numbering/legal traceability implies at least an audit trail of auto-created invoices.
reporting.evidence: Context: legal numbering rules + audit expectations on financial documents.

risks.value: Risk of duplicate/incorrect invoices if trigger fires on re-signature or amendment, or if source data is incomplete at signature time.
risks.evidence: Inferred from context's recurring-trap notes on idempotency and incomplete source data.

questions.q: Does one contract always produce exactly one invoice, or can billing be milestone-based, recurring/subscription, or partial (one-to-many)?
questions.why: high impact x empty: this decides whether we build a single-document trigger or a full billing schedule engine — the two are very different scopes.

questions.q: Where does the invoice amount, VAT rate, currency and numbering come from at signature time — the contract's stated value, a pricing table, or manual entry afterward?
questions.why: high impact x empty: an invoice is never 'just created'; without a defined amount source the automation cannot produce a valid legal document.

questions.q: Should invoice creation happen synchronously at signature (blocking, must succeed or the signature action fails) or asynchronously (queued, with a visible pending state until it completes)? And how do we prevent a duplicate invoice if the contract is re-signed or amended?
questions.why: high impact x empty: sync vs async changes error handling and UX entirely; idempotency prevents costly duplicate/incorrect invoices.

questions.q: Once auto-created, who can see, edit, validate or cancel the invoice before it's sent — is it created as a draft for finance review, or issued directly?
questions.why: high impact x empty: permissions are routinely missed until UAT; an auto-issued (vs draft) invoice has very different risk and rights implications.

questions.q: Is this auto-invoicing rule the same for every client/contract type, or does it need to be configurable (e.g. some clients bill manually, others by milestone)?
questions.why: high impact x empty: the platform's billing rules are typically client-specific — hardcoding a single rule risks rework across the client base.

summary.objective: Automatically generate an invoice when a contract reaches 'signed' status, removing manual delay and errors in triggering billing.
summary.scope: Likely scope: subscribe to the contract-signature event; determine invoice amount/terms from the contract (or a billing schedule if one-to-many); create the invoice in draft or issued state depending on review needs; ensure idempotency against re-signature or amendments; route visibility and notifications to the right roles; apply French VAT and legal numbering rules.
assumptions: Today invoicing is done manually by finance/admin after being informed of a signature.
One contract produces one invoice unless stated otherwise (needs confirmation given one-to-many billing patterns).
The generated invoice will need at least a review/draft step before being sent to the client.
summary.blind_spot: The request assumes 'signed → invoice' is a single, simple trigger, but signature is only the starting event: the amount source, numbering, one-vs-many billing (milestones/recurring), sync-vs-async execution, and idempotency on re-signature/amendment are all undefined and each changes the build significantly.

problem.value: Eliminate manual, delayed invoice creation after contract signature to speed up billing and reduce errors.
problem.evidence: Client wants automatic creation triggered by signature; no explicit pain point stated.

current_process.value: Unknown - presumably someone manually creates the invoice today after being told a contract is signed.

actors.value: Likely: signatory triggers the event, finance/admin role owns the resulting invoice.
actors.evidence: Standard platform roles; not confirmed for this flow.

business_objects.value: Contract and Invoice are the two objects involved; invoice needs amount source, numbering, VAT, payment terms - none specified.

business_rules.value: Unclear how the invoice amount/schedule is derived - full contract value, first milestone, or recurring subscription lines.
business_rules.evidence: Context: a contract is one-to-many with invoices (milestones, recurring, partial billing) - request implies a single simple creation.

workflow.value: Trigger event = contract signature (explicit). Sync vs async handling, and idempotency on repeated signature events, not addressed.
workflow.evidence: Signature is named as the trigger; execution mode and duplicate-prevention are undefined.

integrations.value: Likely needs to notify finance/client contact once invoice is created; channel and recipient unspecified.
integrations.evidence: Not mentioned; inferred from domain notes on notification routing.

permissions.value: Unclear who may view/edit/cancel the auto-generated invoice.
permissions.evidence: Not addressed; permissions are a known recurring blind spot in this domain.

config_vs_custom.value: Unknown whether the trigger/amount rule is uniform or varies by client/contract type/country.
config_vs_custom.evidence: Platform is configurable multi-client; approval/billing rules typically vary by client or contract.

constraints.value: French VAT and legal invoice numbering rules must be respected by any auto-generated invoice.
constraints.evidence: Context: invoicing follows French VAT and legal numbering rules - a regulatory constraint on this exact feature.

edge_cases.value: What if contract data needed for invoicing (amount, VAT, billing contact) is incomplete at signature? What if signature event fires twice (duplicate invoice)?
edge_cases.evidence: Context flags idempotency and incomplete source data as classic traps for 'create X automatically' requests.

reporting.value: Some traceability likely needed (who/what triggered auto-creation) given legal numbering/VAT requirements.
reporting.evidence: Regulatory invoicing constraints imply at least basic audit trail, though not explicitly requested.

risks.value: Risk of duplicate or incorrect invoices if trigger fires multiple times or on incomplete contracts.
risks.evidence: Inferred from domain traps around automatic creation.

questions.q: When the contract is signed, what determines the invoice amount and schedule - the full contract value, a specific milestone, or a recurring/subscription line? Can one contract require several invoices?
questions.why: High impact/high uncertainty: a contract is one-to-many with invoices in this domain; 'create an invoice' could mean one document or an entire billing schedule, which is a fundamentally different build.

questions.q: Should invoice creation happen synchronously (blocking, the user sees success/failure immediately) or asynchronously (queued, with a pending state and retries)? And if the signature event fires twice, must we guarantee only one invoice is created (idempotency)?
questions.why: High impact/high uncertainty: sync vs async is a different error-handling and UX build, and duplicate-invoice risk is a common trap for 'do Y automatically' triggers.

questions.q: Who should be able to see, edit or cancel the auto-created invoice - finance only, the contract's manager too, or the external client contact?
questions.why: High impact/high uncertainty: permissions are consistently forgotten until acceptance testing and touch multiple existing screens.

questions.q: Does the invoicing rule (amount source, timing, numbering) vary by client, contract type, or country, or is it identical for every contract on the platform?
questions.why: High impact/high uncertainty on a configurable multi-client platform: a client- or country-specific rule changes this from a hardcoded trigger to a configurable rules engine.

questions.q: What should happen if the contract is missing data needed to invoice (amount, VAT rate, billing contact) at the moment of signature - block signature, create a draft invoice for review, or fail silently?
questions.why: High impact/high uncertainty: incomplete source data is a named recurring trap for automatic-creation triggers and determines whether a manual fallback/review step is needed.

summary.objective: Automatically generate an invoice when a contract is signed, removing a manual step between signature and billing.
summary.scope: Likely scope: subscribe to the contract's signed event, derive invoice data (amount, VAT, numbering, payment terms) from the contract, create the invoice in a draft or issued state, and surface it to the appropriate role - plus safeguards against duplicate creation and incomplete source data.
assumptions: Today invoice creation after signature is a manual step performed by an admin/finance user.
One signed contract may need to produce more than one invoice depending on billing model (milestone/recurring/partial).
The invoicing rule may differ by client, contract type or country given the platform's configurability.
Basic traceability of auto-created invoices is expected given French VAT/legal numbering constraints.
summary.blind_spot: The request assumes 'create an invoice' is a single, simple action, but a contract is one-to-many with invoices in this domain (milestones, recurring, partial billing) - and nothing is said about idempotency (duplicate signature events), incomplete contract data at signature time, or who is allowed to see/edit the result. Any of these can silently turn one automated trigger into duplicate or non-compliant invoices.

problem.value: Eliminate manual delay/errors in issuing an invoice after a contract is signed.
problem.evidence: Request implies a manual step today ('we want it created automatically').

current_process.value: Unknown — presumably someone manually creates the invoice after signature today, using contract terms.
current_process.evidence: Not stated; inferred from the automation request.

success_metrics.value: Unstated — likely 'no invoice missed or delayed after signature'.

actors.value: Signatory event triggers the process; finance/admin currently issues invoices manually; an approver may need to validate before the invoice is sent.
actors.evidence: Contract signature and invoicing are both named; roles not specified.

business_objects.value: Contract and Invoice named. Missing: amount source, numbering sequence, payment terms, VAT/currency, which contract fields feed the invoice.
business_objects.evidence: Explicit: 'contract', 'invoice'. Rest inferred from domain notes.

business_rules.value: Unknown: is it one invoice for the full contract amount, or does the contract bill via milestones/recurring/subscription (contract is one-to-many with invoices)? Does the rule vary by client/contract type/country?
business_rules.evidence: Context: 'a contract is one-to-many with its invoices... invoice the contract is rarely a single document'.

workflow.value: Trigger = contract signature event. Unknown: synchronous (blocking, must succeed inline) vs asynchronous (queued, retried, visible pending state); initial invoice status (draft awaiting validation vs auto-issued).
workflow.evidence: Context: signature is an event other modules subscribe to; sync/async is rarely specified and changes the build.

integrations.value: Who or what is notified once the invoice is created (finance team, client contact by role, accounting system)? Not specified.
integrations.evidence: Not mentioned; inferred need per domain notes on notification routing by contact role.

permissions.value: Unknown who can view/edit/cancel/override an auto-created invoice, and whether validation is required before it becomes official.
permissions.evidence: Context flags permissions as 'forgotten until acceptance testing'; invoicing carries legal numbering/VAT so access matters.

config_vs_custom.value: Unknown whether this automation is standard for all contracts/clients or configurable/exception-driven per client or contract type.
config_vs_custom.evidence: Platform is configurable multi-client; business rules explicitly vary by client/contract/country in this domain.

constraints.value: French VAT and legal invoice numbering rules must be respected by any auto-generated invoice.
constraints.evidence: Context: 'invoicing follows French VAT and legal numbering rules'.

edge_cases.value: Unhandled: re-signature/amendment re-triggering (idempotency — avoid duplicate invoices), incomplete contract data (missing amount/terms), cancelled or re-opened contracts.
edge_cases.evidence: Context explicitly names idempotency and incomplete source data as recurring traps for 'create X automatically'.

reporting.value: Not stated whether auto-created invoices need to be flagged/traceable (e.g. 'auto-generated' marker, log of the triggering event).
reporting.evidence: Not explicit, but invoicing/legal numbering context raises baseline above default low.

risks.value: Risk of duplicate or incorrect invoices being sent automatically, or invoices generated on incomplete data reaching clients.
risks.evidence: Inferred from domain traps around automatic creation and idempotency.

questions.q: When the contract is signed, should the invoice cover the full contract amount, or does the contract's billing structure (milestones, recurring/subscription, partial billing) mean several invoices need to be scheduled over time?
questions.why: uncertainty×impact: high — a contract is one-to-many with invoices; this decides whether we build one document or a whole billing schedule engine.

questions.q: Should invoice creation happen synchronously right when signature completes (blocking, must succeed or roll back visibly), or asynchronously as a queued/eventual process with a 'pending' state and retries — and should it start as draft (needing validation) or be auto-issued?
questions.why: uncertainty×impact: high — sync vs async changes error handling, UX and cost, and the initial status changes who can still intervene.

questions.q: Does this automatic-invoice rule apply identically to every client/contract type, or does it need to be configurable (enabled per client, per contract type, or per country)?
questions.why: uncertainty×impact: high — configurable platform; billing rules here commonly vary by client/contract/country, changing this from a hardcoded trigger to a rules engine.

questions.q: What happens if the contract is re-signed (e.g. via an amendment) or the signature event fires again — should this ever create a second invoice? And what if required billing data (amount, terms) is missing or incomplete at signature time?
questions.why: uncertainty×impact: high — idempotency and incomplete source data are named traps for 'create X automatically'; unresolved, this causes duplicate or broken invoices in production.

questions.q: Once auto-created, who can see, edit, approve or cancel the invoice before it's sent to the client — is a validation step required, or does it go out untouched?
questions.why: uncertainty×impact: high — permissions are typically forgotten until acceptance testing, yet an auto-issued invoice with legal numbering/VAT implications carries real financial consequences if uncontrolled.

summary.objective: Automatically generate an invoice the moment a contract is signed, removing manual delay and error in the handoff between contract signature and billing.
summary.scope: Likely scope: subscribe to the contract-signature event; derive invoice amount, numbering, payment terms, VAT and currency from the contract; create the invoice (draft or issued) possibly per a milestone/recurring schedule rather than a single document; guard against duplicate creation on re-signature/amendment; route visibility/notification to the right roles (finance, client billing contact); respect legal numbering and VAT rules.
assumptions: Today the invoice is created manually after signature (current_process, inferred).
Roles involved include a signatory event trigger, a finance/admin creator, and possibly an approver (actors, inferred).
The trigger is the contract-signature event, but sync vs async is not yet decided (workflow, inferred).
French VAT and legal invoice numbering rules apply to the generated invoice (constraints, inferred).
summary.blind_spot: The request assumes 'invoice the contract' is a single, simple document creation. In this domain a contract is one-to-many with its invoices (milestones, recurring billing, partial billing), and re-signature or amendments can re-trigger the automation — risking duplicate invoices unless idempotency and the billing schedule are explicitly designed.
